title.en: Link Aggregation: Reading the Standard's Scope and Conformance Map
title.zh: 链路聚合：读懂标准的范围与一致性地图

blocks.en: Every IEEE 802 standard opens with two short clauses that tell you what the document is for (Clause 1, the Overview) and what an implementation must actually do to claim it follows the rules (Clause 5, Conformance). For IEEE Std 802.1AX-2020 — the standard for Link Aggregation — these clauses are your map of the whole specification. This lesson walks through them so you can read the rest of the standard fluently. Link Aggregation (LAG) is the technique of bundling several parallel point-to-point links so the layer above treats them as one fatter, more resilient link.
blocks.zh: 每一份 IEEE 802 标准都以两个简短的子句开篇：一个告诉你这份文档的用途（第 1 章，概述 Overview），另一个说明一个实现到底要做到什么才能声称符合规则（第 5 章，一致性 Conformance）。对于 IEEE Std 802.1AX-2020——链路聚合标准——这两章就是你阅读整份规范的地图。本课逐段讲解它们，帮助你顺畅地读懂标准其余部分。链路聚合（Link Aggregation，LAG）是把多条并行的点对点链路捆绑在一起的技术，使上层把它们当作一条更宽、更具弹性的单一链路来对待。

blocks.en: Clause 1.1 (Scope) defines exactly what the standard delivers: protocols, procedures, and managed objects for three things. First, bundling one or more parallel point-to-point links into a Link Aggregation Group (LAG) so a Link Aggregation Client can use the group as if it were a single link. Second, Conversation-Sensitive Collection and Distribution (CSCD) — a way to name the algorithm that decides which link each frame rides on, and to tell the System at the far end which algorithm is in use. Third, the Distributed Resilient Network Interconnect (DRNI) — letting a LAG terminate on a pair of cooperating Systems so you get resilience at the system level, not just the link level.
blocks.zh: 第 1.1 节（范围 Scope）准确界定了标准提供的内容：为三件事提供协议、过程和受管对象。第一，把一条或多条并行的点对点链路捆绑成一个链路聚合组（Link Aggregation Group，LAG），使链路聚合客户端（Link Aggregation Client）能把整组当作单一链路使用。第二，对话敏感的收集与分发（Conversation-Sensitive Collection and Distribution，CSCD）——一种命名“决定每个帧走哪条链路”的算法、并把所用算法告知对端系统的方法。第三，分布式弹性网络互连（Distributed Resilient Network Interconnect，DRNI）——让一个 LAG 终结于一对协作的系统上，从而在系统层面（而不仅仅是链路层面）获得弹性。

blocks.en: Clause 1.2 (Purpose) explains the why. Aggregating links gives you higher total bandwidth than any single member link, lets you use multiple systems at each end of the aggregation, and improves utilization in Bridged local area network (LAN) environments. Just as importantly, it improves resilience: if an individual link or system fails, the LAG keeps carrying traffic over what remains. When the LAG connects separately administered networks, each network is also shielded from the other's fault-recovery events — a failure on one side does not ripple across as churn on the other.
blocks.zh: 第 1.2 节（目的 Purpose）解释“为什么”。聚合链路带来的总带宽高于任何单条成员链路，允许在聚合的每一端使用多个系统，并改善桥接局域网（Bridged LAN）环境中的链路利用率。同样重要的是，它提升弹性：当某条链路或某个系统失效时，LAG 会继续用剩余资源承载流量。当 LAG 连接的是分别管理的网络时，每个网络还能与对方的故障恢复事件相互隔离——一侧的故障不会作为抖动波及另一侧。

blocks.en:
flowchart LR
  C[Link Aggregation Client]
  C --> A[Aggregator]
  A --> L1[Link 1]
  A --> L2[Link 2]
  A --> L3[Link 3]
  L1 --> P[Partner System]
  L2 --> P
  L3 --> P
blocks.zh:
flowchart LR
  C[链路聚合客户端]
  C --> A[聚合器]
  A --> L1[链路 1]
  A --> L2[链路 2]
  A --> L3[链路 3]
  L1 --> P[对端系统]
  L2 --> P
  L3 --> P

blocks.en: Clause 1.3 (State diagram conventions) is the reading guide for the protocol machinery later in the standard. 802.1AX borrows the state-diagram conventions from Annex E of IEEE Std 802.1Q-2018. The key rules to remember: where a state diagram is required for conformance, every state and transition drawn with a solid line shall be implemented, while those drawn with dashed lines may be implemented (they are optional). And if the diagram ever disagrees with the matching transition table or the prose, the state diagram wins — it is the authoritative source.
blocks.zh: 第 1.3 节（状态图约定 State diagram conventions）是阅读标准后文协议机制的指南。802.1AX 沿用 IEEE Std 802.1Q-2018 附录 E 的状态图约定。需要记住的关键规则：当某个状态图为一致性所必需时，凡用实线绘制的状态与转移都“应”（shall）实现，而用虚线绘制的“可”（may）实现（它们是可选的）。并且，若状态图与对应的转移表或文字描述发生冲突，以状态图为准——它是权威来源。

blocks.en: Solid line = mandatory, dashed line = optional. And when text, table, and diagram disagree, the state diagram is the tie-breaker. Keep this in mind whenever you reach the LACP or DRCP state machines deeper in the standard.
blocks.zh: 实线 = 必选，虚线 = 可选。当文字、表格与图三者不一致时，以状态图作为裁决依据。在标准后文遇到 LACP 或 DRCP 状态机时，请始终记住这一点。

blocks.en: Clause 5 (Conformance) is where the standard becomes testable. Clause 5.1 (Requirements terminology) pins down the verbs. Shall marks a mandatory requirement. May marks an implementation or administrative choice — "is permitted to," so "may" and "may not" mean exactly the same freedom to choose. Should marks a recommended (but not required) choice. The standard also reserves is / are for definitions and logical consequences, can for permitted behavior that is neither always required nor directly controlled, and cannot for behavior that never occurs in a conformant implementation. Every shall, may, and should in the document is mirrored in the Protocol Implementation Conformance Statement (PICS) proformas in Annex A.
blocks.zh: 第 5 章（一致性 Conformance）是标准变得可检验的地方。第 5.1 节（需求术语 Requirements terminology）确定了这些动词的含义。“应”（shall）表示强制要求。“可”（may）表示实现或管理上的选择——即“被允许”，因此“may”和“may not”表达的都是同样的“可自由选择”。“宜”（should）表示推荐但非强制的选择。标准还约定：is / are 用于定义和逻辑后果，can 用于既非总是必需、也不受直接控制的被允许行为，cannot 用于在一致性实现中绝不发生的行为。文档中每一处 shall、may、should 都映射到附录 A 的协议实现一致性声明（Protocol Implementation Conformance Statement，PICS）形式表中。

blocks.en: Clause 5.2 (Protocol implementation conformance statement) states the supplier's duty directly: anyone claiming conformance shall identify both the supplier and the implementation, and shall fill out a copy of the PICS proforma in Annex A. The PICS is the checklist that turns the standard's scattered "shall" and "may" statements into a single auditable declaration of what a product does and does not support — the bridge between writing a standard and proving a box obeys it.
blocks.zh: 第 5.2 节（协议实现一致性声明 PICS）直接规定了供应商的义务：任何声称符合标准的一方“应”同时标识供应商与实现，并“应”填写附录 A 中 PICS 形式表的一份副本。PICS 是一张清单，把标准中分散的 shall 与 may 条款汇成一份可审计的声明，说明产品支持与不支持什么——它是“写出标准”与“证明设备遵从标准”之间的桥梁。

blocks.svg: <svg viewBox="0 0 440 150" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" fill="none" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="2"><rect x="10" y="20" width="120" height="40"/><text x="70" y="45" text-anchor="middle" stroke="none" fill="currentColor" font-size="13">shall (mandatory)</text><rect x="10" y="70" width="120" height="40" stroke-dasharray="5 4"/><text x="70" y="95" text-anchor="middle" stroke="none" fill="currentColor" font-size="13">may (optional)</text><line x1="130" y1="40" x2="230" y2="40"/><line x1="130" y1="90" x2="230" y2="90" stroke-dasharray="5 4"/><rect x="230" y="45" width="200" height="60"/><text x="330" y="70" text-anchor="middle" stroke="none" fill="currentColor" font-size="13">PICS proforma</text><text x="330" y="90" text-anchor="middle" stroke="none" fill="currentColor" font-size="11">(Annex A)</text></svg>

caption.en: Mandatory (solid) and optional (dashed) requirements both flow into the PICS proforma, the supplier's auditable conformance checklist.
caption.zh: 强制（实线）与可选（虚线）要求都汇入 PICS 形式表——供应商可审计的一致性清单。

blocks.en: Clauses 5.3 and 5.4 split the conformance requirements into two capability tiers. 5.3 covers plain Link Aggregation, and 5.4 covers the more advanced DRNI. Each tier separates what a System must do (the "requirements" sub-clauses, using shall) from what it may do (the "options" sub-clauses, using may). This lesson highlights the two options sub-clauses, 5.3.2 and 5.4.2, because they are the menu of features an implementer can pick from on top of the mandatory baseline — and they reveal how DRNI is built as a strict superset of basic Link Aggregation.
blocks.zh: 第 5.3 节与第 5.4 节把一致性要求分为两层能力。5.3 涵盖普通的链路聚合，5.4 涵盖更高级的 DRNI。每一层都把系统“必须做”的部分（用 shall 的“要求 requirements”子节）与“可以做”的部分（用 may 的“选项 options”子节）分开。本课重点讲解两个选项子节——5.3.2 与 5.4.2——因为它们是实现者在强制基线之上可挑选的功能菜单，也揭示了 DRNI 如何被构建为基本链路聚合的严格超集。

summary.en: Coverage map: 5.3.2 Link Aggregation options and 5.4.2 DRNI options, clause by clause
summary.zh: 覆盖地图：逐条解读 5.3.2 链路聚合选项与 5.4.2 DRNI 选项

blocks.en:
Clause 5.3.2 (Link Aggregation options) lists what a conformant Link Aggregation System may add on top of the mandatory baseline in 5.3.1: (a) the Marker Protocol (state machines in 6.5, Marker and Marker Response Protocol Data Units in 6.5.3); (b) the management functionality of Clause 7; (c) SMIv2 management information base (MIB) modules (Annex D); (d) Aggregation Port Debug Information package support (7.2.5); (e) conformance to the Link Layer Discovery Protocol (LLDP) of IEEE Std 802.1AB, where a System that supports LLDP shall provide an LLDP Parser/Multiplexer per F.1; (f) a Protocol Parser/Multiplexer (6.1.3) for a protocol not otherwise specified; (g) the link-grouping techniques of 6.7.1 and 6.7.2; (h) Conversation-Sensitive Collection and Distribution (6.2.7 and 6.6), where support requires a FORCE_FALSE value for Admin_Discard_Wrong_Conversation and may add FORCE_TRUE and AUTO; and (i) Per-Service Frame Distribution (8.2), which itself requires CSCD, version 2 LACPDUs (6.4.2.4), and classification by Customer/Service VLAN tag, and may add Backbone Service Instance Tag classification (8.2.3).

Clause 5.4.2 (DRNI options) builds on the DRNI requirements in 5.4.1. A conformant DRNI System may (a) implement any of the Link Aggregation options from 5.3.2 — so every basic option carries forward; (b) use a LAG for the Intra-Relay Connection (IRC) by supporting the Intra-Relay Port (IRP) with a dedicated Aggregator (9.3); (c) use a method other than a dedicated link or LAG for the IRC (9.3); and (d) support Organization-Specific TLVs in Distributed Relay Control Protocol Data Units (DRCPDUs) per 9.6.2.7. Notice the layering: DRNI conformance (5.4.1a) first requires full Link Aggregation conformance (5.3), then adds the Distributed Relay, Distributed Relay Control Protocol (DRCP), and DRCPDU machinery — and its option list explicitly inherits 5.3.2. That is the standard's structural backbone: Overview (1.1–1.3) frames the scope, terminology (5.1) and the PICS (5.2) make it testable, and the requirement/option pairs (5.3, 5.4) layer DRNI cleanly on top of Link Aggregation.
blocks.zh:
第 5.3.2 节（链路聚合选项）列出一致性链路聚合系统在 5.3.1 强制基线之上“可”增加的内容：(a) 标记协议 Marker Protocol（状态机见 6.5，标记与标记响应协议数据单元见 6.5.3）；(b) 第 7 章的管理功能；(c) SMIv2 管理信息库（management information base，MIB）模块（附录 D）；(d) 聚合端口调试信息包支持（7.2.5）；(e) 符合 IEEE Std 802.1AB 的链路层发现协议（Link Layer Discovery Protocol，LLDP），支持 LLDP 的系统“应”按 F.1 提供 LLDP 解析/复用器；(f) 为标准未规定的协议实现一个协议解析/复用器（6.1.3）；(g) 6.7.1 与 6.7.2 的链路成组技术；(h) 对话敏感的收集与分发（6.2.7 与 6.6），支持时 Admin_Discard_Wrong_Conversation 需取值 FORCE_FALSE，并可增加 FORCE_TRUE 与 AUTO；(i) 逐业务帧分发 Per-Service Frame Distribution（8.2），它本身要求 CSCD、版本 2 的 LACPDU（6.4.2.4）以及按客户/业务 VLAN 标签分类，并可增加按骨干业务实例标签分类（8.2.3）。

第 5.4.2 节（DRNI 选项）建立在 5.4.1 的 DRNI 要求之上。一致性 DRNI 系统“可”：(a) 实现 5.3.2 中任何链路聚合选项——因此所有基本选项都延续下来；(b) 通过用专用聚合器支持内部中继端口（Intra-Relay Port，IRP）来为内部中继连接（Intra-Relay Connection，IRC）使用一个 LAG（9.3）；(c) 为 IRC 使用专用链路或 LAG 以外的方法（9.3）；(d) 在分布式中继控制协议数据单元（Distributed Relay Control Protocol Data Unit，DRCPDU）中支持组织特定 TLV（9.6.2.7）。注意这种层叠关系：DRNI 一致性（5.4.1a）首先要求完整的链路聚合一致性（5.3），再加上分布式中继、分布式中继控制协议（Distributed Relay Control Protocol，DRCP）及 DRCPDU 机制——其选项清单明确继承 5.3.2。这正是标准的结构主干：概述（1.1–1.3）框定范围，术语（5.1）与 PICS（5.2）使之可检验，而要求/选项成对的章节（5.3、5.4）把 DRNI 干净地层叠在链路聚合之上。

cites.note: Clause 1.1 Scope and 1.2 Purpose (p.19), 1.3 State diagram conventions (p.20), 5.1 Requirements terminology and 5.2 PICS and 5.3 Link Aggregation (p.26), 5.3.2 Link Aggregation options and 5.4 DRNI (p.27), 5.4.2 DRNI options (p.28).
